target.title: fix(imessage): gate split-send coalescing on imsg metadata
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Replaces iMessage split-send merge decisions with the new `imsg` structural URL-balloon metadata from openclaw/imsg#137.
- Preserves `balloon_bundle_id` at the OpenClaw iMessage RPC payload boundary.
- Merges an opt-in coalesce bucket only when one source row carries `balloon_bundle_id: "com.apple.messages.URLBalloonProvider"`.
- Flushes buckets without that marker as separate messages, so older `imsg` builds and ordinary rapid DMs are not merged from short-text or URL-shape heuristics.

Why does this matter now?

- The previous OpenClaw workaround depended on guessing from short messages before another row. The corresponding `imsg` PR gives OpenClaw a structural signal instead.

What is the intended outcome?

- `Dump` + URL-preview split-sends can still become one turn when the installed `imsg` exposes the URL-balloon field.
- Fieldless payloads, including older `imsg` output, do not enable structural split-send merging.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No fallback heuristic for old `imsg` builds.
- No non-URL attachment split-send merge, because the new `imsg` field only proves URL preview balloon rows.

What does success look like?

- URL-balloon buckets merge; fieldless buckets flush separately; docs describe the metadata requirement and latency tradeoff.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the merge gate is at the right iMessage monitor boundary and whether docs accurately describe the dependency on openclaw/imsg#137.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90795

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related openclaw/imsg#137

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested by maintainer in chat after closing #90795 in favor of the `imsg` contract fix.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: iMessage DM split-send coalescing now uses `imsg` `balloon_bundle_id` metadata instead of short-message/string-shape inference.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout with the iMessage monitor running through mocked `imsg` JSON-RPC notifications; sibling `imsg` source inspected directly for the output-field contract.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/parse-notification.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts`
  - `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers && pnpm check:changelog-attributions && pnpm lint:extensions:no-guarded-wildcard-reexports && pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports && pnpm dup:check:coverage && pnpm deps:pins:check && pnpm deps:patches:check && pnpm tsgo:extensions && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test && pnpm lint:extensions && pnpm check:media-download-helpers && pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders && pnpm check:import-cycles`
  - `pnpm format:docs:check && pnpm docs:check-mdx`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 34 tests; direct changed-check equivalent passed; docs format and MDX checks passed; autoreview was clean after addressing one docs wording finding.
- Observed result after fix: monitor test proves a fieldless `Dump` + URL bucket dispatches as two turns, while the same bucket with `balloon_bundle_id: "com.apple.messages.URLBalloonProvider"` dispatches as one merged turn.
- What was not tested: live OpenClaw against a released `imsg` containing openclaw/imsg#137, because that `imsg` PR is not landed/released yet.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:changed` could not run through its normal Testbox delegation because local Crabbox failed sanity checks; running the child mode locally then failed because `corepack` is not on this host PATH. The direct command list above is the same changed-check plan for the selected extension/docs lanes.
- Before evidence: old behavior merged coalesced buckets without structural `imsg` URL-balloon metadata.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/coalesce.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/parse-notification.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers && pnpm check:changelog-attributions && pnpm lint:extensions:no-guarded-wildcard-reexports && pnpm lint:extensions:no-plugin-sdk-wildcard-reexports && pnpm dup:check:coverage && pnpm deps:pins:check && pnpm deps:patches:check && pnpm tsgo:extensions && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test && pnpm lint:extensions && pnpm check:media-download-helpers && pnpm check:runtime-sidecar-loaders && pnpm check:import-cycles`
- `pnpm format:docs:check && pnpm docs:check-mdx`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Parser test for preserving `balloon_bundle_id`.
- Coalesce helper test for URL-balloon structural recognition.
- Monitor notification-path tests for fieldless bucket no-merge and URL-balloon bucket merge.
- Existing live-catchup cursor test updated so the coalesced bucket uses the structural marker.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- OpenClaw could merge based on coalesced bucket timing/text shape without proving the later row was a URL balloon from `imsg`.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Opt-in iMessage split-send coalescing now requires `imsg` URL-balloon metadata before merging.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No config shape or migration change. Existing `channels.imessage.coalesceSameSenderDms` remains the switch.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Older `imsg` builds no longer get heuristic split-send merging; they wait for the debounce window and then flush rows separately.

How is that risk mitigated?

Docs call out the current-`imsg` metadata requirement, and tests prove fieldless payloads do not merge.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review after openclaw/imsg#137 lands or while reviewing both PRs as a paired change.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI plus live proof against an `imsg` build that includes openclaw/imsg#137.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Autoreview finding about stale lone-URL latency docs was fixed; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
target.author: omarshahine
